message:
test(e2e): add FCP compliance E2E test suite for 19 connectors

Build out the E2E compliance test infrastructure to cover all major
connectors in the flywheel ecosystem.

fcp-e2e crate changes:
- Add 17 new feature flags gating per-connector E2E tests: anthropic,
  slack, discord, google_ai, qdrant, telegram, pinecone, plaid, github,
  gmail, linear, stripe, notion, youtube, twitter, google_calendar, s3
- Wire all connector crates as feature-gated dev-dependencies
- Add toml workspace dependency for manifest TOML parsing in tests
- Refactor lib.rs: rename openai_manifest_with_hash() to generic
  reference_manifest_with_hash() loading from test vectors, replace
  CapabilityId::from_static with .parse::<CapabilityId>(), decouple
  host_allowed() from ConnectorManifest struct via raw TOML parsing,
  restructure streaming_sse_body() with serde_json construction
- Add inline Slack E2E test module (~520 lines) covering default-deny,
  valid token invoke, network guard, cross-capability denial, and
  read-vs-write receipt distinction

New compliance E2E test binaries (crates/fcp-e2e/tests/):
- anthropic_compliance_e2e.rs
- discord_compliance_e2e.rs
- github_compliance_e2e.rs
- gmail_compliance_e2e.rs
- google_ai_compliance_e2e.rs
- google_calendar_compliance_e2e.rs
- linear_compliance_e2e.rs
- notion_compliance_e2e.rs
- openai_compliance_e2e.rs
- pinecone_compliance_e2e.rs
- plaid_compliance_e2e.rs
- qdrant_compliance_e2e.rs
- s3_compliance_e2e.rs
- slack_compliance_e2e.rs
- stripe_compliance_e2e.rs
- telegram_compliance_e2e.rs
- twitter_compliance_e2e.rs
- youtube_compliance_e2e.rs

Each test binary exercises the full FCP compliance suite: handshake,
capability verification, invoke with valid/invalid tokens, network
guard host validation, and streaming backpressure where applicable.
All tests use wiremock for HTTP mocking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
